decks.notes: This deck focuses on playing aggressive red creatures and burn spells to quickly deplete the opponent's life total. Utilizing a combination of haste creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with efficient burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike, the goal is to apply constant pressure and finish off the opponent before they can stabilize. The inclusion of Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provides additional late-game reach, allowing the deck to continue threatening the opponent even after they stabilize.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive creature threats like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while utilizing powerful burn spells like Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage to control the board and deal direct damage. The deck also includes card advantage options like Academic Dispute and Stock Up to maintain hand advantage and keep up the pressure. The deck's mana base is carefully constructed to ensure consistency and flexibility in casting spells, with a focus on efficiency and speed.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with instants and sorceries to bring back Arclight Phoenix, utilizing cards like Treasure Cruise and Sleight of Hand to draw into more spells. It also includes removal spells like Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse to control the board. Ledger Shredder and Picklock Prankster provide additional graveyard synergy while cards like Opt and Consider help with card selection. The deck aims to be fast and aggressive, using the recurring threat of Arclight Phoenix to pressure opponents and close out the game quickly.

The deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field as a key piece for generating mana quickly to enable the combo of playing Scapeshift to search for specific lands and win the game. It also includes cards such as Wandering Minstrel and Arboreal Grazer to accelerate ramping. The deck aims to set up a strong mana base and control the board while drawing cards to assemble the combo pieces efficiently, allowing for a powerful finish by casting Scapeshift.

The key focus of this Dimir Control deck is to maintain board control and disrupt the opponent's strategy through a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and versatile threats. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Boomerang Basics, the deck aims to keep the opponent on the back foot while building towards powerful late-game plays with cards like Yorion, Sky Nomad and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares. The deck also includes a variety of lands to ensure consistent mana access and options for different game states.

This deck focuses on using a combination of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful engine that can overwhelm opponents quickly. Thoughtseize helps disrupt the opponent's game plan, while cards like Iron-Shield Elf and Fatal Push provide protection and removal. The deck also includes various lands to support the combo, with Monument to Endurance providing additional value. The strategy is to control the game early on and then quickly ramp into the combo pieces to secure victory.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang + Parhelion II to generate a powerful win condition. It includes cards that facilitate the combo like Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance, as well as disruption and removal spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game until the combo can be assembled. The deck also includes various lands to support the strategy and ensure consistent mana production. Overall, the key strategy is to efficiently set up and execute the Greasefang Parhelion combo to overwhelm the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize aggressive Spirit creatures to put pressure on the opponent while disrupting their game plan with counter spells and removal. The deck aims to control the board and tempo by keeping the opponent on the back foot while continuously applying pressure with evasive creatures. The selection of cards allows for a versatile playstyle, enabling the player to adapt to different matchups and strategies. The key strategy is to maintain a balance between aggression and control to secure victory in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck is focused on playing aggressively with a mix of burn spells and creature threats. The key strategy is to quickly build a board presence with low-cost creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear while using burn spells like Fiery Impulse to control the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. Stormchaser's Talent and Cori-Steel Cutter provide additional value by buffing creatures and providing card advantage. The deck aims to close out the game quickly by overwhelming the opponent with fast, efficient threats.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards like Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance to support this combo, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to control the board. Thoughtseize is included for disruption and Cling to Dust for graveyard hate. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the combo pieces and ensure consistency in executing the game plan.

The primary focus of this deck is to utilize Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II in combination with Monument to Endurance to create a powerful combo that can overwhelm opponents. With support from cards like Fleeting Spirit and Guardian of New Benalia, the deck aims to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy while building towards its combo finish. The mana base is optimized to support the combo pieces and provide flexibility in casting spells. In essence, this deck seeks to assemble its key cards and unleash a devastating combo for victory.

This UW Control deck focuses on controlling the board through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It utilizes cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, Supreme Verdict, and Dovin's Veto to control the game. The deck also includes card draw spells like Memory Deluge and Consult the Star Charts to ensure it has the resources it needs to outlast opponents. The deck aims to disrupt opponents' strategies while building towards a win condition, whether it's through creature beats or a powerful planeswalker ultimate ability.

The main idea behind this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent using a combination of cheap spells and creatures with prowess. By utilizing cards like Burst Lightning, Soul-Scar Mage, and Monastery Swiftspear, the deck aims to apply pressure early on and maintain tempo throughout the game. With a mix of burn spells and aggressive creatures, the UR Aggro deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize and take control of the game.

The main concept of this deck is to assemble the combo pieces of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy that can overwhelm the opponent. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push are included for disruption and removal, while Monument to Endurance provides support for the combo pieces. The deck also features a mix of lands to support the color requirements of the cards and to ensure consistency in mana production. Overall, the deck aims to set up its combo quickly and efficiently to secure victory in the Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with spells to bring back Arclight Phoenixes from the graveyard, generating a powerful and aggressive board presence. Cards like Consider, Fiery Impulse, Sleight of Hand, and Lightning Axe help with card selection and graveyard filling, while cards like Picklock Prankster and Profit's Eidetic Memory assist in returning Arclight Phoenix to the battlefield. The deck also includes efficient removal and card draw spells to maintain momentum and pressure the opponent. The mana base is carefully constructed to enable consistent casting of spells and creatures.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. It aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total by deploying fast creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger, while also using burn spells like Lightning Strike and Burst Lightning to deal direct damage. The deck also incorporates cards like Den of the Bugbear and Kumano Faces Kakkazan to generate additional threats and value. Overall, the strategy of this deck is to apply constant pressure and finish the game before the opponent can stabilize.
